name: Fuente Caputa and Rambla de Perea Loop
name_original: La Fuente Caputa — Circular por la Rambla de Perea
description: Hike the easy 3.8-mile La Fuente Caputa circular route through diverse landscapes, badlands, and an oasis with a waterfall and natural pools
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Sierra de Ricote are characterized by a diverse landscape shaped by the Segura River, which irrigates fertile plains and orchards. The region features rugged mountains, cliffs, and unique geological formations like badlands. Pine forests provide shaded paths, contrasting with the more open, arid mountainous terrain. Best family friendly hiking trails around Sierra de Ricote The most popular family friendly hiking route is Scenic Valley View loop from Ulea, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This easy route offers gentle gradients and scenic views across the valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Azud de Ojós Reservoir – Azud de Ojós Reservoir loop from Blanca, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path. This trail follows the reservoir, providing views of the water and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Ojós, a 1.6 miles (2.6 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 40 minutes. Hiking around Sierra de Ricote is defined by river valleys, fertile orchards, and rugged hills, offering a blend of natural and agricultural scenery. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Sierra de Ricote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 250 reviews. More than 1,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierra de Ricote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Our guide features over 60 family-friendly hiking routes in Sierra de Ricote, ranging from easy strolls to more moderate adventures. You'll find a diverse selection to suit various ages and activity levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The trails in Sierra de Ricote offer a wonderful variety of terrain. You can expect paths that wind through lush fruit and citrus orchards, alongside the Segura River, and through more arid mountainous areas with unique geological formations like badlands. Many routes also feature pine forests, providing welcome shade.

Q: Are there any easy, short hikes suitable for families with young children?
A: Yes, there are several easy options perfect for younger children. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Ojós&lt;/a&gt; is a short, easy route. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Valley View loop from Ulea&lt;/a&gt;, which offers gentle elevation and lovely views.

Q: Can we find trails with waterfalls or natural pools in the area?
A: Absolutely! The region is home to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2651129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of an oasis with natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14623717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cierva Reservoir – Fuente Caputa Waterfall loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that allows you to experience this natural wonder.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Many of the family-friendly trails in Sierra de Ricote are circular, making them convenient for day trips. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Valley View loop from Ulea&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38236880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Ojós Reservoir loop from Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied scenery without retracing your steps.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or viewpoints can we see along the trails?
A: Sierra de Ricote is rich in natural beauty and offers stunning viewpoints. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de la Pila&lt;/a&gt; or the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ricote Valley from Collado de la Calera&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Solvente loop from Ojós&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically designed to showcase panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any trails that are suitable for strollers?
A: While many trails feature natural surfaces, some sections through orchards or along river paths might be suitable for robust, all-terrain strollers. For very young children, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Ojós&lt;/a&gt;, with its minimal elevation, could be considered, but always check the specific trail conditions before heading out.

Q: What is the best time of year for family hiking in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for family hiking in Sierra de Ricote. The weather is milder, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be very hot, so if you hike then, choose early mornings or late afternoons and look for trails with shade, such as those passing through pine forests or along the river.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the family-friendly trails?
A: Many trails in Sierra de Ricote are dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your furry companions along. However, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to ensure you clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family-friendly trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The komoot community highly rates the family-friendly trails in Sierra de Ricote, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the Segura River, and the well-maintained paths that make for enjoyable outings with children. The presence of natural features like the Fuente Caputa Waterfall is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads for family hikes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near the starting points of many popular family-friendly trails in Sierra de Ricote, particularly in and around towns like Ulea, Ojós, and Blanca. We recommend checking the specific route details on komoot for information on designated parking areas.
tips: In this small oasis, next to the Sierra de Ricote and just over 10 kilometers from Mula, two Roman sites are preserved, related to the use of water, which document an important settlement of this area during the Roman Empire.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The La Fuente Caputa — Circular by the Rambla de Perea trail is enjoyable year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, while the natural pools are particularly inviting for swimming during the warmer summer months.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is largely classified as easy and is suitable for all fitness and skill levels, making it a great option for families and beginners. While most paths are easily accessible, some rocky sections within the rambla may require sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Fuente Caputa — Circular by the Rambla de Perea trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, ranging from well-maintained dirt and tarmacked tracks to more rugged, rocky sections within the rambla. You'll encounter impressive calcareous walls, narrow canyons, and a large gully with soft, erosive rocks characteristic of a semi-arid landscape. Appropriate footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers captivating scenery, including lush pine groves, 'lunar scenery' known as badlands, and unique geological formations. A major highlight is the Fuente Caputa Waterfall, an oasis with natural pools perfect for swimming. You can also discover remnants of Roman water management systems, the Perea Aqueduct, and areas of archaeological significance.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking lots are not detailed, the trail is located near Mula in Murcia and is known for its easily accessible paths. Parking is generally available near the main access points to the Rambla de Perea, which serves as the trailhead for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on this trail. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and to ensure you clean up after them. Be mindful of rocky sections and potential heat during warmer months.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the La Fuente Caputa — Circular by the Rambla de Perea trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail features several natural pools, or 'pozas,' along the watercourse, particularly around the Fuente Caputa Waterfall. These pools, with their clear, sometimes bluish water, are popular for swimming, especially during the summer.

faqs.question: What kind of historical or geological features can I find here?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in history and geology. You'll find Roman sites related to water management, evidence of Neanderthal settlements over 60,000 years old, and historic transhumance routes. Geologically, the Rambla de Perea boasts 'sublime geodiversity' with formations spanning 70 million years, earning it recognition as a Site of Community Importance.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this hike, we recommend bringing plenty of water, especially in warmer months, appropriate mountain footwear for rocky sections, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a swimsuit if you plan to enjoy the natural pools. A camera is also a good idea to capture the diverse scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Mula, a town in Murcia. While there might not be facilities directly at the trailhead, you can find cafes and restaurants in Mula itself, which is a short drive away, offering options for refreshments before or after your hike.
